content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES

OWEN A. SILVERMAN, INC.
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION
23224 CRENSHAW BOULEVARD
TORRANCE, CALIFORNIA 90505

(213) 530-3990
(213) 775-8421

OWEN A. SILVERMAN
STEVEN R. BERNARDINO
EARL M. HYMAN

FAX (213) 530-5523

ROBIN MAISEL
IRWIN S. GEVURTZ

August 28, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J. Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Attn.: Kevin Bratten-Moen

In Re: Socialist Workers Campaign Committee

Dear Mr. Bratten-Moen

I am writing in reference to the Socialist Workers Campaign
Committee and to memorialize the substance of our discussions by
telephone on August 21, 1990.

Socialist Workers Party candidates will be running for
office in the 1990 general elections. Supporters of the
Socialist Workers campaign are establishing a Socialist Workers
Campaign Committee. That committee will be raising money and
making expenditures in support of candidates endorsed by the SWP
who are running on the platform and program of the SWP.

Such a committee, according to our conversation, would best
be classified at this time as a broad support recipient
committee. It will not be a controlled committee of any
candidate. Decisions on the kind of material it prints in
support of any candidates, etc., will not be decided by any
candidate and no candidate shall exercise any substantial
control or influence over the committee. Naturally, the
committee will consult with candidates concerning their views,
their availability to attend meetings and other events, etc.

Each SWP candidate for elected office in California will
make his/her own formal declaration of candidacy with your
office by filing for 501. As we discussed over the phone, none
of the candidates plans to raise any money or make expenditures
for their individual candidacies at this time. Therefore,
instead of filing form 502 and opening a bank account, each will
submit a statement to that effect indicating that they might
receive contributions "in kind" in the form of payment of any
filing fees or costs for printing a statement of qualifications
for the voters' pamphlet. Those contributions will come from
the Socialist Workers Campaign Committee and will be reported by
the candidate or the committee at the proper time and in an
appropriate form as your office may require and advise.

Page 2.
SWCC/FPPC
August 28, 1990


     On behalf of the Socialist Workers Campaign Committee I
thank you for your patient and considerate assistance and
cooperation in helping us follow the rules and regulations of
the FPPC.  Should there be questions in the future, we look
forward to working with you.

     Any written correspondence should be addressed to me c/o of
the Socialist Workers Campaign Committee, 2546 W. Pico Blvd.,
Los Angeles, CA  90006.

                              Very Truly Yours,

                              [signature]

                              Robin Maisel
                              Co-Chairperson, Socialist Workers Campaign Committee


cc:  Barry Fatland, Treasurer, SWCC

RM:bt

sections.question_synthetic: How should the Socialist Workers Campaign Committee be classified under FPPC regulations, and what filing requirements apply to SWP candidates who do not plan to raise money or make expenditures for their individual candidacies?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an outgoing correspondence from a legal representative to FPPC staff, not an FPPC advice letter response. It memorializes a prior telephone conversation and does not contain formal Q&A sections. The document appears to be seeking confirmation of verbal guidance previously provided, rather than requesting new advice. No formal FPPC response or conclusion is present in this document.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: How should the Socialist Workers Campaign Committee be classified under FPPC regulations, and what filing requirements apply to SWP candidates who do not plan to raise money or make expenditures for their individual candidacies?

embedding.summary: This is a letter from Robin Maisel (attorney for the Socialist Workers Campaign Committee) to FPPC staff memorializing a telephone discussion regarding the proper classification and filing procedures for a broad-based support committee established to raise funds and make expenditures for Socialist Workers Party candidates in the 1990 general elections.
